summary: Add the `rule` artifact IR (`.claude/rules/*.md` ‚Äî optional `paths` frontmatter + byte-faithful body) and its `extract::rule_features` projection into the engine's `Features` view.

new.description: The `rule` artifact IR (one artifact kind per module, `.claude/rules/rust.md`), mirroring `src/skill.rs`. Parse a `.claude/rules/<name>.md` source into a typed `Rule`: an OPTIONAL `paths` frontmatter sequence (rules may carry NO frontmatter at all ‚Äî collaboration.md has none, rust.md has `paths:` ‚Äî unlike a skill, which errors without frontmatter), unknown frontmatter keys preserved verbatim in an `extra` map (so a `forbidden_keys` clause can later resolve `description`/`globs`/`alwaysApply`), a byte-faithful body, and provenance (`source_path` + SHA-256 `import_hash`); the name derives from the file stem. Ship the surface round-trip too ‚Äî `from_source_dir` plus `from_surface_dir`/`to_meta_document` (header carries `paths` if present + `[provenance]`) ‚Äî so RULE-IMPORT can write and `Workspace::load` reconstruct it. The split-frontmatter/sha256 helpers may be lifted from or duplicated off `skill.rs` (code's choice ‚Äî clarity over a lifetime fight, `.claude/rules/rust.md`).

edit.description: add `pub mod rule;` in alphabetical position (after `pub mod import;` lib.rs:22, before `pub mod skill;` lib.rs:23). This entry is the sole owner of the lib.rs line for the rule kind ‚Äî no sibling entry co-edits it.

add `pub fn rule_features(&Rule) -> Features` mirroring `skill_features` (extract.rs:94): `id` = the rule name (file stem), the `paths` value as a `FeatureValue::List`, every `extra` frontmatter key folded into `fields` (so `forbidden_keys` resolves by name like it does for skills), `body_lines` from the byte-faithful body; `source_dir`/`companions` populated as for skills (uniformity ‚Äî the rule contract names neither). Add a unit test exercising a `paths`-only rule and a no-frontmatter rule.

tests.asserts: a rule with `paths:` frontmatter parses its list; a rule with no frontmatter parses to an empty header + byte-faithful body; an `extra` key (e.g. `description`) is preserved; the source/surface round-trip is identity
`rule_features` exposes `paths` as a list, folds `extra` keys into `fields` so `has_field("description")` is true when present, and counts body lines

acceptance: `temper::rule::Rule` parses both the frontmatter and no-frontmatter rule shapes with a byte-faithful body + provenance; `extract::rule_features` projects it into `Features`; `cargo test`/`clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Foundation of the rule slice (inbox NEW SLICE). `(contract-selection)` is RESOLVED, so no fork blocks this. Surface topology for rules (the `rules/<name>/` layout, body filename) is code-owned mechanism (`90-spec-system.md` depth rule) within law 5's invariants ‚Äî RULE-IMPORT pins it.

summary: Pin the shipped `contracts/rule.toml` template's clause vector, mirroring `tests/contract_template.rs` for the skill template.

new.description: Mirror `tests/contract_template.rs`: load `contracts/rule.toml` (human-authored, already on disk) via `Contract::load` off `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` and assert the exact typed clause vector ‚Äî `optional` on `paths` (advisory), `forbidden_keys = ["description", "globs", "alwaysApply"]` (required), `max_lines = 200` (advisory) ‚Äî at their declared severities, with the display label derived to `rule` from the file stem. A second test asserts only decidable predicates appear (the kind set), documenting that the rule template, like the skill one, carries no semantic guess.

tests.asserts: `contracts/rule.toml` loads into exactly the optional-paths / forbidden-keys / max-lines clause vector at the declared severities; label derives to `rule`

acceptance: The shipped rule template round-trips into the typed `Contract` clause vector; `cargo test`/`clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Independent and immediately pickable ‚Äî depends only on `contracts/rule.toml` (shipped a6497ec) and `contract::Contract` (shipped). Build EMBEDS rule.toml later (RULE-CHECK); contracts/ stays human territory.

summary: Teach `import` to scan `<harness>/.claude/rules/*.md` and write the rule surface (header + byte-faithful body + `[[rule]]` roll-up); `Workspace` gains `rules` and reconstructs them on load.

edit.description: Extend discovery + write to the rule kind. Scan `<harness>/.claude/rules/*.md` (today `discover_skill_dirs` scans only `<harness>/skills/`, import.rs:97), and for each rule write the surface mirroring skills (`run` import.rs:78, `import_skill` import.rs:131): a format-preserving header (optional `paths` + `[provenance]`, via `Rule::to_meta_document`) and the byte-faithful body under a `rules/<name>/` tree, plus a `[[rule]]` row in `author.toml` beside `[[skill]]` (`write_rollup` import.rs:170). Keep the keystone idempotence invariant (import.rs:12).

`Workspace` gains `rules: Vec<Rule>` (struct check.rs:34) and `Workspace::load` reconstructs them from `<workspace>/rules/*/` via `Rule::from_surface_dir`, name-sorted, mirroring the skill load (check.rs:47-74). Update the `Workspace { skills: Vec::new() }` test literal (check.rs:334) for the new field.

tests.asserts: a harness with `.claude/rules/*.md` writes a rule surface + a `[[rule]]` roll-up row; re-import is byte-identical (idempotent); skills and rules coexist in one import
`Workspace::load` reconstructs rules (name-sorted) from the written surface alongside skills

acceptance: `temper import` over a harness carrying rules produces a round-trippable rule surface + roll-up, and `Workspace::load` reads it back; `cargo test`/`clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Rules scan at `<harness>/.claude/rules/*.md` per the spec literal ‚Äî note the root asymmetry with skills (`<harness>/skills/`); self-host (RULE-CHECK) therefore points `import` at the repo root, where `.claude/rules/` lives. Surface topology (the `rules/<name>/` tree, body filename) is code's faithful generalization of the skill layout under law 5.

summary: Dispatch `check` by artifact kind ‚Äî embed `contracts/rule.toml`, validate `ws.rules` against it, merge with skill diagnostics; prove self-host on `temper`'s own `.claude/rules/`.

per.section: Decision: contract selection is by artifact kind

edit.description: Dispatch `check` by kind. Embed the rule contract beside the skill one: `const BUILTIN_RULE_CONTRACT: &str = include_str!("../contracts/rule.toml");` (next to `BUILTIN_SKILL_CONTRACT` main.rs:30). In the `Check` arm (main.rs:77-82), project `ws.rules` via `extract::rule_features`, validate them against the rule contract parsed by `Contract::parse`, and merge the rule diagnostics with the skill diagnostics before `check::render` / the `any_error` + `--deny-advisories` gate (main.rs:84-94) ‚Äî both contracts run, each artifact judged by the contract for its kind.

Add a rule-kind case: a harness whose `.claude/rules/` holds a clean rule (e.g. `paths:`-only) checks clean, and one holding a forbidden Cursor key (`description`/`globs`/`alwaysApply`) exits non-zero. Add the self-host case: `temper import <repo-root>` (off `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR`) then `temper check` over `temper`'s OWN `.claude/rules/*.md` (rust.md carries `paths:`, collaboration.md none) exits zero ‚Äî the bootstrap proof.

tests.asserts: import->check over a harness with rules exits zero on a clean rule, non-zero on a forbidden-key rule; self-host: import the repo root then check exits zero over temper's own `.claude/rules/`

acceptance: `temper check` validates skills against the skill contract and rules against the rule contract in one run; `temper check` runs green on temper's own `.claude/` (`specs/00-intent.md` self-hosting proof); `cargo test`/`clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Closes the inbox NEW SLICE. The merged-diagnostics gate keeps `--deny-advisories`/`any_error` semantics unchanged. Self-host is the first real bite of `00-intent.md`'s bootstrap finish line; the SessionStart gate that refuses to start on a failing harness is later work.
